ai_research.headline: SpaceX nails 600th rocket landing with final GPS III satellite — Tesla earnings tomorrow: the $400 question

items.title: SpaceX GPS III-8 launch success — 600th rocket landing milestone
items.body: Falcon 9 launched GPS III-8 ('Hedy Lamarr') from Cape Canaveral at 2:53 AM EDT. First stage landed on droneship — SpaceX's 600th rocket landing. Final satellite in the GPS III series (Lockheed Martin), enhancing accuracy and anti-jamming. Originally assigned to ULA Vulcan, reassigned to SpaceX in March after Vulcan delays. Satellite now maneuvering to operational orbit.

items.title: Tesla Q1 earnings tomorrow — key metrics to watch
items.body: Q1 earnings April 22 after close. Revenue forecast: ~$21.2B. Key metrics: Robotaxi unit economics (3 TX cities now fully driverless), Megapack deployments (8.8 GWh last reported, energy division projected 25% revenue growth), FSD subscription numbers, and any Terafab chip timeline updates. Stock at $392.50 — was $352 a month ago, $400 on Friday. Energy division is the sleeper story offsetting declining auto margins.

items.title: Google expected to unveil new inference-focused TPU chips this week
items.body: Google reportedly preparing new TPU announcements focused on inference optimization, challenging NVIDIA's dominance. Comes as Meta-Broadcom custom chip deal and Terafab accelerate the custom silicon trend. Inference hardware is the new battleground as AI deployment scales.

ai_research.learn_today: SpaceX's 600th landing is a milestone that highlights the reliability gap — ULA lost the GPS III-8 contract because Vulcan couldn't deliver on schedule. In the space industry, consistency beats innovation. Same lesson applies to agents: the boring stuff (reliability, uptime, cost control) matters more than the flashy demos.

openclaw_agents.headline: Competitor landscape hardening — Kimi Claw launches, MyClaw hits pricing backlash, agents getting real-world scrutiny

items.title: Kimi Claw (Moonshot AI) — browser-based agents with OpenClaw integration
items.body: Moonshot AI launched Kimi Claw: browser-based agents, one-click cloud deploy, K2 model, OpenClaw integration. Getting compared to Claude Code. Biggest competitor buzz right now. This is the 'agent-as-a-platform' wave hitting.
items.try_this: Worth watching how Kimi Claw positions against OpenClaw's model — they're integrating rather than competing, which might be the right play for the ecosystem phase.

items.title: MyClaw's pricing transparency problem — users hit with 10x cost spikes
items.body: Users complaining about auto model upgrades causing 10x cost spikes without warning. Pricing transparency is becoming a differentiator. If you're building agent products, clear cost controls and upgrade notifications are table stakes now.
items.try_this: This is an angle to exploit if InstaClaw or OpenClaw has transparent pricing. The market punishes surprise bills harder than it rewards features.

items.title: ILO adopts first-ever conclusions on AI in manufacturing
items.body: International Labour Organization released guidelines on AI's role in manufacturing — first time global body has formalized positions on AI job impacts and ethical deployment. Signals that agent/automation regulation is accelerating at the international level.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Tesla earnings tomorrow after close. If you hold TSLA, set an alert. Robotaxi economics and energy storage growth are the two stories that matter for the long thesis.

tesla.note: Flat ahead of Q1 earnings tomorrow (April 22, after close). Robotaxi now in 3 TX cities (Austin, Dallas, Houston). Energy storage division key offset to declining auto margins. Stock pulled back from Friday's $400 — market pricing in some uncertainty. Revenue forecast: ~$21.2B.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: GPS III-8 launched successfully — 600th rocket landing. Final GPS III satellite deployed to MEO. Also: NASA FY2027 budget cuts threaten $175.7M ESA Mars rover contract. SpaceX IPO still confidentially filed, NASDAQ bending rules for inclusion. Musk's cryptic 'Ascension' post from yesterday still unexplained.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: No standalone news. Still dealing with French probe over X/Grok content issues. Terafab chip project continues in background — Intel partnership for 2nm production targeting 2029.

items.title: Unchosen (2026)
items.note: Drops today. 6 episodes. Cult drama — young mother from a sheltered cult has a risky affair, uncovers dark secrets. Stars Asa Butterfield. Could be the next 'Wild Wild Country' vibe but scripted.

items.title: Nosferatu
items.note: Hits Peacock today. If you missed it in theaters, this is the Robert Eggers vampire film. Gorgeous, atmospheric, worth your time if you like horror with craft.

items.title: Untold: The Shooting at Hawthorne Hill
items.note: True crime doc — deadly dispute between a dressage trainer and student ending in bloodshed and an insanity defense. The Untold series has been consistently good.

items.title: Criminal Record — Season 2
items.note: Drops tomorrow. Peter Capaldi and Cush Jumbo return. If you liked Season 1's London crime thriller vibes, S2 is an easy pick.

items.title: Competitor watch: MyClaw pricing backlash is an opening
items.body: MyClaw users are angry about 10x cost spikes from auto model upgrades without warning. If OpenClaw or InstaClaw has transparent pricing, this is the moment to lean into it. 'You'll never get a surprise bill' is a winning message when competitors are fumbling it.

items.title: Tesla earnings tomorrow — if QuizzyDots/InstaClaw has any Tesla angle, hold for Wednesday
items.body: Earnings after close April 22. Robotaxi expansion and energy storage are the stories. If there's any Tesla-related content to publish, wait for the earnings bump (or drop) for maximum attention.

macro.mood: Markets quiet pre-earnings. TSLA flat at $392 ahead of tomorrow's Q1 report. BTC pushing $76.7K (+2%). LMND holding $68. Google inference TPU announcement expected this week — could move chip stocks. All eyes on Tesla after close tomorrow.
